name: Hearty Minestrone-Eating Well
recipeIngredient: 4 Cups Reduced Sodium Chicken Broth, defatted
1 Large Red Potato, scrubbed and diced
1/2 Teaspoon Freshly Ground Black Pepper, or to taste
15 Ounces White Beans, drained and rinsed
2 Zucchini, trimmed, 1/4 sliced, then thinly sliced
recipeInstructions:
1. In a large pot or Dutch oven, heat olive oil over medium-high heat.
2. Add the leeks and cook, stirring occasionally, until soft, about 3-4 minutes.
3. Add the broth and 1 cup water. Add potatoes, thyme, salt, and pepper. Bring to a boil.
4. Reduce heat to low and let simmer for 5 minutes, covered.
5. Add the orzo and cook, stirring to prevent sticking, for 5 minutes.
6. Add the beans and zucchini. Cook, partially covered, for about 8 more minutes or until pasta and veggies are tender.
7. Stir in the spinach and cook for 2 more minutes.
8. Season with vinegar. Ladle into bowls and sprinkle with cheese.
Makes 8 cups. Serves 4 as a main course
385 calories per serving
22 grams protein
6 grams fat
67 grams carbohydrates
510 mg sodium
2 mg cholesterol
